replies.author: @randytravis5893
replies.text:
dose your graphine ink transfer heat well? I like your idea of graphine, fluid heat transfer. I bet it would be much more cost efficient to use that idea than to grow billions of graphine fibers to create a cable. And if his cable ever snapped or got cut, that's it, you have to pull up the entire thing and even might have to redig, but you can just seal a leak or send down a probe to fix the leak and refill your graphine fluid, so much easier. If you don't mind, would it be alright with you, if I could mention your name to his web sight? I will let him know you make graphine and do your own graphine research. I will be respectful, and not go on a tangent, and leave your youtube channel so he can check out your research. Its amazing how so many talented people never seem to meet each other, I would like to think your research and his goals would go well together, but I will leave that up to you, and if you think its ok, he will contact you.
Cant wait to see what you come up with next.
Did you ever get to do a ballistic test on your graphine impregnated plastic?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Randy Travis yeah mate - no worries and go for it - Steve is working on the plastic right now as it happens

replies.author: @randytravis5893
replies.text:
Cool! im so excited about that! That could make a bullet proof vest that is lighter than water. When I was wearing my body armor, in the army, one of my biggest fears was falling into a stream or body of water and getting dragged down to my death, by drowning. If it works well, please swing your idea by to darpa or the us army, im sure they would love to pay you for your formula or give a generous donation to your research.
You guy's are the best! Keep up the good work, some one must be taking notice of you by now, I hope you are getting lots of donations for your research, you deserve so much more,
I think you must be the best math teacher ever! Wish I had you, when I was in high school, but I figure we are pretty close in age, so that's just silly, but I suspect I would have pared up with you for study hall, and read your notes, lol.
I will leave a message to Mr. Bhargava. I hope he will contact you.
All the best.
Randy.

author: @charmorff
text:
I suspect it is acting like a germanium diode ( crystal radio).
What would be more interesting is?
Connecting the output to a Voltage Cockroft Walton multiplier.
This can be used to pulse change a 12 volt PB Battery ;-)
If successful? 
It will require a 741 op amp set as a comparator,  as not to over charge the battery.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: give it a go and let me know how it works out

replies.author: @josephellsworth4537
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith   You don't need a Cockroft multiplier to make this useful.  In fact you have the reverse problem of reducing the voltage enough to effectively charge the battery circuit without burning the power off as heat in the diodes.        I built a circuit using a AC rectifier and some smoothing capacitors that does this pretty well.   I was using it to charge a 3.6V circuit.  I tested with a 10,000uf storage capacitor which was charged to 5.1V over a 6 hour period.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Joseph Ellsworth that's very cool - do you have a circuit diagram you would share?

replies.author: @josephellsworth4537
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith Could not add a image here so I made it available at https://drive.google.com/file/d/0B4wv6y9tmhTgRThzSjNrVUd6cVU  you may want to double check my math before trusting.   


I was thinking about building a small PCB and software for STM32L0538-DISCO to display mW of charge received in a near realtime basis,  mWh of charge since test start,  mWh of charge per hour so you can see the difference as you test the device in different areas.  You should also be able use the same device to measure your monoTherm and I could add a temp sensor so you can log the temperature correlation to power output.    


Let me know if you think your channel subscribers would be interested in this as a kit.  It would be a good excuse to make a new PCB and test new paper-white display on the STM part for under $30.00.


The STM part could accumulate a few hundred readings but you would need to connect to a PC via USB to download the readings.  Could also add a 1 Megabit storage chip to allow logging over longer period of time.     I think we would need to add a LED blink to keep the storage cap under 5V which makes the voltage dividers easy or could extend it with a user settable voltage limit and allow it to charge a external CAP or battery up to that limit.   If using to charge an external cap may want to also allow it to charge the battery we run the CPU from so it will run forever provided you are supplying sufficient current with a AC wave above 3V.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Joseph Ellsworth I am in the US at the moment mate - but i will discuss this with Ashley when i get back and we will have a look at your circuit - at first glance it looks pretty cool - if you are up for it we might do avid on it and point the viewers to a link to your kit?

Are there any important advice how to make the sheet. Thank you!

text: Great Job Robert, I see in this particular case it appears that it's picking up the Frequency in the Atmosphere? But when it comes to your Hand I'm wondering if there is more to it than just your Hand being a airial?
